description: Vata/Pitta imbalance often causes tension or throbbing headaches. A simple cooling or warming application helps depending on the dosha.
remedies: Vata: Apply a warm paste of dry ginger powder and water to the forehead.
Pitta: Apply a cool paste of sandalwood powder or coriander leaves to the forehead.
Drink warm water with a pinch of cumin and coriander seeds.
precautions: Avoid ginger paste if the skin is highly sensitive or if the headache is Pitta (burning) type.

description: Migraines are typically Pitta-dominant but involve Vata pushing Pitta into the head.
remedies: Instill 2 drops of warm cow's ghee in each nostril (Nasya).
Drink a tea made from coriander seeds, cumin seeds, and fennel seeds.
Apply a paste of nutmeg (Jaiphal) rubbed with water on the forehead.
precautions: Consult an Ayurvedic practitioner if migraines are frequent and severe. Do Nasya only on an empty stomach.

description: Dry cough indicates a Vata imbalance in the respiratory tract.
remedies: Chew a piece of fresh ginger with a pinch of salt.
Mix 1 tsp honey, a pinch of black pepper, and a pinch of turmeric. Take twice daily.
Gargle with warm water and sesame oil.
precautions: Do not give honey to infants under 1 year old. Avoid cold foods.

description: Productive or wet cough indicates Kapha accumulation.
remedies: Mix 1 tsp honey with 1/2 tsp sitopaladi churna or ginger juice.
Drink tea made of tulsi (holy basil) and ginger.
Steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil or ajwain (carom seeds).
precautions: Avoid dairy products and cold drinks as they increase Kapha.

description: Inflammation in the throat, often Pitta and Kapha related.
remedies: Gargle with warm water containing 1/2 tsp turmeric and 1/2 tsp salt.
Slowly chew a clove (Lavanga) or cardamom pod.
Drink warm milk with a pinch of turmeric at bedtime (Golden Milk).
precautions: Avoid sour and extremely spicy foods.

description: Fever (Jwara) is considered the 'king of diseases' in Ayurveda, originating in the gut (Amashaya).
remedies: Fasting (Langhana) or drinking only warm rice water (Peeya).
Decoction of Guduchi (Giloy) stem or Tulsi leaves.
Drink water boiled with coriander seeds and dry ginger (Shadanga Paniya).
precautions: Do not suppress fever with cold water baths. Seek medical help if fever exceeds 102°F or lasts >3 days.

description: A classic Pitta imbalance (Amlapitta) where digestive fire goes out of control.
remedies: Drink 1/2 cup of cool milk or fresh coconut water.
Chew 1/2 tsp of fennel seeds (Saunf) after meals.
Mix 1 tsp of coriander seed powder and 1 tsp of raw sugar in water and drink.
precautions: Avoid spicy, sour, fermented, and deep-fried foods. Avoid eating right before bed.

description: Vata accumulation in the colon causing dryness and hard stools.
remedies: Take 1 tsp of Triphala powder with warm water before bedtime.
Drink 1 glass of warm milk with 1 tsp of cow's ghee at night.
Soak a handful of raisins in water overnight and eat them first thing in the morning.
precautions: Avoid dry, cold, and raw foods. Ensure adequate hydration.

description: Vata imbalance causing trapped wind, or low digestive fire (Agnimandya).
remedies: Chew a slice of fresh ginger with a pinch of rock salt 10 minutes before meals.
Drink warm water with 1/2 tsp of ajwain (carom seeds) and a pinch of black salt.
Apply a paste of asafoetida (Hing) mixed with warm water around the navel.
precautions: Avoid heavy beans, cabbage, and cold water during meals.

description: Atisara (diarrhea) is a Pitta or Vata condition causing excess liquid discharge.
remedies: Drink buttermilk (Takra) mixed with a pinch of roasted cumin powder and rock salt.
Take a paste of 1/2 tsp grated nutmeg (Jaiphal) with honey.
Eat mashed boiled rice with yogurt.
precautions: Maintain hydration with ORS or coconut water. If bloody or lasts >2 days, see a doctor.

description: Vata imbalance in the mind causing restless thoughts and inability to sleep.
remedies: Massage the soles of the feet with warm sesame oil or Brahmi oil before bed.
Drink a cup of warm milk with a pinch of nutmeg and cardamom.
Practice deep breathing (Pranayama) or Shavasana.
precautions: Avoid caffeine after 2 PM. Avoid excessive screen time before bed.

description: Excess Vata causing instability in the mind (Manasika Dosha).
remedies: Drink Ashwagandha tea or take 1/2 tsp Ashwagandha powder with warm milk.
Apply warm Brahmi oil to the scalp.
Practice Nadi Shodhana (Alternate Nostril Breathing).
precautions: Maintain a regular daily routine (Dinacharya) to ground Vata.

description: Vata lodging in the joints causing dryness and pain (Sandhigata Vata).
remedies: Massage the affected joint with warm Mahanarayana oil or sesame oil.
Apply a warm paste of ginger powder and water to the joint.
Take 1/2 tsp of turmeric and a pinch of black pepper with warm water.
precautions: If the joint is red, hot, and swollen (Pitta/Gout), avoid heating oils and use cool sandalwood paste instead.

description: Pitta and Kapha imbalance leading to blood impurity (Rakta dushti).
remedies: Apply a paste of neem powder and rose water to the pimples.
Apply a paste of sandalwood powder and turmeric.
Drink 1 tsp of aloe vera juice twice a day on an empty stomach.
precautions: Avoid spicy, oily, and heavily fermented foods. Do not pop pimples.

description: Vata accumulation in the lower abdomen (Apana Vayu).
remedies: Drink warm water with 1 tsp of crushed cumin seeds.
Apply a warm castor oil pack or sesame oil to the lower abdomen.
Drink a tea made of ginger and fennel seeds.
precautions: Rest during the first 2 days of the cycle. Avoid cold foods and intense exercise.
